title: How HRA Exemption is Calculated: The Actual Formula
description: HRA exemption is the minimum of three values — most salaried employees claim less than they're entitled to. Here's the exact formula, worked examples for metro and non-metro cities, and when HRA beats home loan interest.

title: Gratuity Calculation Formula in India: What You'll Actually Receive
description: Gratuity uses a simple formula but most employees don't know what counts as 'last drawn salary' or how the 5-year eligibility rule really works. Here's the exact calculation with worked examples.

title: EPF Interest Calculation: How PF Interest is Calculated Each Year
description: PF interest is calculated monthly but credited annually — and the way it's compounded surprises most employees. Here's exactly how your EPF balance grows, with the 2025-26 interest rate of 8.25%.

title: Capital Gains Tax in India FY 2025-26: Rates, Exemptions, Examples
description: Budget 2024 changed STCG to 20% and LTCG to 12.5% with ₹1.25 lakh exemption. Here's how capital gains tax applies to equity, mutual funds, property and gold in FY 2025-26.
target_keyword: india short term capital gains tax

title: CTC to In-Hand Salary: Why Your Take-Home is Much Less Than CTC
description: A ₹12 lakh CTC offer doesn't mean ₹1 lakh monthly take-home. After PF deductions, income tax, professional tax and gratuity provisions, the gap is usually 20-30%. Here's the exact breakdown.

title: RD Interest Calculation: How Recurring Deposit Interest Works
description: RD interest uses quarterly compounding per RBI rules — not simple interest as most people assume. Here's the actual formula, why banks show different maturity amounts, and when RD beats FD.

title: PPF Maturity Amount: How to Calculate What Your PPF Will Be Worth
description: PPF compounds annually at a rate set by the government every quarter — currently 7.1%. The 15-year lock-in and partial withdrawal rules catch most investors off guard. Here's how to calculate your actual maturity amount.

title: TDS on Salary: How Your Employer Calculates and Deducts Tax
description: Your employer deducts TDS from salary every month based on projected annual income — but most employees don't know how to check if the deduction is correct. Here's how TDS on salary is calculated.

title: Home Loan Prepayment: How Much Interest You Actually Save
description: Prepaying ₹1 lakh on a ₹40 lakh home loan in year 3 saves you roughly ₹2.4 lakh in interest over the remaining tenure. Here's the math behind prepayment and when it makes more sense than investing.

title: Reverse GST Calculation: How to Find Base Price from GST-Inclusive Amount
description: Most people calculate GST the forward way — add 18% to a price. Reverse calculation (finding the base price from a GST-inclusive bill) uses a different formula that's easy to get wrong.

title: NPS vs PPF: Which Retirement Saving Option Wins for Salaried Employees
description: NPS gives an extra ₹50,000 deduction under 80CCD(1B) but locks 40% in annuity at retirement. PPF is fully tax-free but returns 7.1%. The right answer depends on your tax slab and retirement timeline.

title: Stamp Duty and Registration Charges: State-by-State Guide for 2026
description: Stamp duty ranges from 3% in Maharashtra for women to 7% in some states for men. Registration charges add another 0.5-1%. On a ₹60 lakh property this can mean ₹4-5 lakh extra — often forgotten in budget planning.

title: Rule of 72: How Long Does It Take to Double Your Money in India
description: Divide 72 by your expected return rate and you get the years to double your money. PPF at 7.1% takes 10 years. Equity at 12% takes 6. It's not precise but it's the fastest mental math in personal finance.

title: CAGR Calculation: What It Actually Means for Mutual Fund Returns
description: CAGR is the single most important number when comparing mutual funds — but most investors read it wrong. Here's the exact formula, why absolute returns mislead on long-term investments, and how to calculate CAGR from NAV history.

title: Compound Interest Calculation: The Formula and Why It Matters More Than You Think
description: Compound interest is calculated on principal plus accumulated interest — not just the original amount. The difference between simple and compound interest on a ₹5 lakh FD over 10 years is over ₹3 lakh.

title: FD vs RD: Which Gives Better Returns for the Same Amount
description: FD and RD both offer fixed returns but compound differently. On ₹6 lakh invested over a year, an FD beats an RD on returns — but RD wins for people without a lumpsum. Here's the exact comparison.
target_keyword: fd vs rd which is better

title: Emergency Fund: How Much You Actually Need and Where to Keep It
description: The standard advice is 6 months of expenses. But 6 months of what — income or spending? And where: savings account, liquid fund, or FD? Here's a practical emergency fund calculation for Indian salaried employees.

title: Net Worth Calculation: How to Calculate Your Net Worth in India
description: Net worth = assets minus liabilities. Simple formula, but most Indians undercount assets (EPF, PPF, gratuity) and undercount liabilities (outstanding credit card, home loan principal). Here's a proper Indian net worth template.

title: 50/30/20 Rule for Indian Salaries: Does It Actually Work?
description: The 50/30/20 rule says 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings. On a ₹60,000 take-home in Mumbai that means ₹30,000 for rent and essentials — nearly impossible. Here's how to adapt the rule for Indian city costs.

title: Inflation and Your Savings: What a 6% Inflation Rate Does to ₹10 Lakh
description: At 6% inflation, ₹10 lakh today is worth ₹5.58 lakh in real terms after 10 years. An FD at 7% barely keeps up. Here's how to calculate the real return on your savings after inflation.

title: Debt-to-Income Ratio: Why Banks Reject Loan Applications in India
description: Your debt-to-income ratio — total EMIs divided by net monthly income — is the number banks look at first. Above 50% (FOIR) and most lenders reject you. Here's how to calculate it and what to do if it's too high.
target_keyword: debt to income ratio calculator india

title: Gold Investment in India: Returns, Forms, and the Real Tax Picture
description: Gold has returned about 13% CAGR in INR over the last 20 years — better than most people think. But the form of gold (physical, SGBs, Gold ETFs) changes both returns and tax treatment significantly.

title: Section 80C Deductions: The Complete List and Which Ones to Actually Use
description: Section 80C allows up to ₹1.5 lakh deduction but has 15 eligible instruments. ELSS, PPF, EPF, LIC, NSC, home loan principal — each has different liquidity, returns, and lock-in. Here's how to pick.

title: Car Depreciation in India: How Much Your Car Loses Every Year
description: A new car loses 15-20% of its value in the first year and 10-15% each year after that. On a ₹10 lakh car, you've lost ₹3.5 lakh by year 3. Here's the depreciation schedule and how it affects insurance and resale value.

title: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana: Returns, Rules, and the Actual Maturity Amount
description: SSY offers 8.2% interest, full tax exemption, and matures when the girl turns 21. On ₹1.5 lakh/year for 15 years starting from birth, the maturity value at age 21 is approximately ₹69 lakh. Here's the full calculation.

title: Sovereign Gold Bond vs Gold ETF: Which is Better for Indian Investors
description: SGBs pay 2.5% interest on top of gold price appreciation and are tax-free on maturity. Gold ETFs are liquid but taxed as debt funds since April 2023. The 8-year lock-in of SGBs is the real trade-off to understand.
target_keyword: sovereign gold bond vs gold etf

title: How to Read Form 16: Part A and Part B Explained Simply
description: Form 16 is issued by your employer every year by June 15. Most employees can't read it correctly — especially Part B where deductions and taxable income are calculated. Here's what every section means.

title: Credit Card Interest Calculation: Why the 3.5% Monthly Rate is Devastating
description: 3.5% per month sounds small. It's 42% per year. On a ₹50,000 unpaid balance, you owe ₹21,000 in interest over a year — more than most personal loans. Here's exactly how credit card interest is calculated.

title: Pay Raise vs Job Switch: The Real Numbers Behind the Decision
description: The average internal increment in India is 10%. The average hike from switching jobs is 25-40%. Over 5 years the gap compounds. Here's how to calculate the lifetime earnings difference between staying and switching.
target_keyword: salary hike after job change india

title: ELSS vs PPF for 80C: Which Tax-Saving Investment is Actually Better
description: ELSS has a 3-year lock-in and targets 12-14% returns. PPF has a 15-year lock-in at guaranteed 7.1%. On a 10-year horizon with reinvestment, ELSS typically wins — but only if you stay invested through the dips.
target_keyword: elss vs ppf which is better

title: Home Loan vs Renting: The Honest Financial Comparison for Indian Cities
description: In Mumbai, a ₹1.5 crore flat on a 20-year home loan costs ₹1.3 lakh EMI. The same flat rents for ₹40,000-50,000/month. The EMI argument assumes the flat appreciates 7%+ annually. Here's when buying actually makes financial sense.

title: Mutual Fund Expense Ratio: How 1% More in Fees Costs You ₹15 Lakh
description: On a ₹50 lakh portfolio over 20 years at 12% returns, the difference between 0.1% (index fund) and 1.5% (active fund) expense ratio is roughly ₹32 lakh. Expense ratio is the most underrated factor in mutual fund selection.

title: Loan Amortization: Why You Pay Mostly Interest in the First Few Years
description: On a ₹40 lakh home loan at 8.75% for 20 years, your first EMI of ₹35,392 includes ₹29,167 in interest and only ₹6,225 in principal. It takes 8 years before your EMI is split 50/50. Here's the full amortization explained.

title: Retirement Corpus Calculation: How Much Do You Actually Need to Retire
description: The 25x rule says you need 25 times your annual expenses to retire. At ₹5 lakh/year expenses today, inflated at 6% for 25 years, you need a corpus of ₹5.4 crore. Here's the full calculation for Indian retirement planning.
